title: D1-004d1 admission ledger core
description:
## Goal and why
D1-004d1 admission ledger core: add the durable Postgres admission ledger foundation — Drizzle migration/schema, admissionLedger.ts, session advisory fence, and CLI destructive-diff read — keyed by host, binding, and execution identity. Commit before effects, use advisory fencing, and fail closed on mismatch or commit failure. This is the reusable admission ledger core; it does not own consumer wiring or rollback journaling.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. ≤400 net lines. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode. Scope is narrowed to the ledger core only: Drizzle migration/schema + admissionLedger.ts + session advisory fence + CLI destructive-diff read. D1-004d2 owns Pi first-effect admission consumers; D1-004d3 owns the WorkspaceBridge operation admission seam. D1-004e owns append-only rollback-journal creation, prepared-state reads, and recovery fencing. D1-005c is the first concrete stable-core consumer of the admission hook.

## Explicit exclusions
No rollback-journal reads or writes, publication pointer, served acknowledgement, scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. No Pi service decorator, createAgent.ts facade, slash/reload hook wiring, or WorkspaceBridge operation wiring (those are D1-004d2/d3).

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit tests prove admission rows and advisory fencing commit before the first effect and reject a mismatch/commit failure without effects. Run targeted typecheck/tests and attach redacted output.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance field: focused proof verifies only this bead's owned contract.

Acceptance Criteria:
Focused proof verifies: admitted binding remains non-removable after process restart and revision-directory cleanup; landing-only revisions preserve execution identity; binding-id reuse with different execution facts fails closed; concurrent admission is idempotent; kill-after-commit-before-effect leaves a deliberate permanent fence; and pre-commit/lock failure produces no effect. No rollback journal or stable-core publication behavior is implemented.

Owned stable codes: D1_ADMISSION_IDENTITY_MISMATCH, D1_ADMISSION_RECORD_FAILED (must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).

Split per sizing audit 2026-07-13; siblings D1-004d2/d3 carry the consumer seams (pre-declared in PR-PLAN).
acceptance_criteria:
Focused proof verifies: admitted binding remains non-removable after process restart and revision-directory cleanup; landing-only revisions preserve execution identity; binding-id reuse with different execution facts fails closed; concurrent admission is idempotent; kill-after-commit-before-effect leaves a deliberate permanent fence; and pre-commit/lock failure produces no effect. No rollback journal or stable-core publication behavior is implemented.

Owned stable codes: D1_ADMISSION_IDENTITY_MISMATCH, D1_ADMISSION_RECORD_FAILED (must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).

title: #391/#805 Decision 28: application Agent fleet, Workspace orchestration, and Environment service
description:
## Context
Decision 28 supersedes the former Decision 26 typed-product/Step-1A graph. This epic now coordinates #391/#805 delivery of one deployment-static AgentApplication fleet, ordinary Workspace-owned persisted defaults and sessions, independent Core/web and CLI consumers, Workspace orchestration, and one governed `boring-bash` Environment service over Agent/Workspace-neutral `boring-sandbox` providers.

## Durable invariants
- Signup hostname initializes only a new Workspace default; it never grants membership, filters a portfolio, routes an existing Workspace, or mutates a persisted default.
- Web membership and CLI trusted-local policy are the only consumer access authorities.
- Workspace owns fleet/default/session/governance/orchestration. Agent owns one service-shaped in-process application. Core and CLI are peers and never boot/route through each other.
- All file/search/watch/exec surfaces use one authoritative Environment API/filesystem; no copy/sync architecture.
- Governance compiles invocation-scoped views; model credentials and shell grants are separate capabilities; providers fail closed when physical requirements are unsupported.
- Expand, migrate, conformance, owner-approved contraction, contracted release-candidate proof, owner publication approval, then exact publication.

## Graph policy
The Decision-28 F-series children are the only dispatchable implementation graph. Former R1-R6/C1-C4/I1 descendants are deferred historical Decision-26 snapshots. Closed R0 and R4 descendants remain closed evidence and are not reimplemented. Every child stays in_progress until its implementation PR merges.

## Authority
Decision 28 in docs/DECISIONS.md; product gates in docs/issues/391/plan.md; normative contracts, dependency edges, proof/review/rollback and Human Intention gates in docs/issues/805/runtime-refactor/work/A1-agent-authoring/WORKSPACE-AGENT-FLEET-PLAN.md.
acceptance_criteria: All Decision-28 children through F8b are closed by merged PR/proof; H2c and H8 contain explicit owner approvals; conditional H4a is either untriggered with clean audit evidence or resolved through an owner-approved graph amendment; closed R0/R4 evidence remains linked; no former Decision-26 child is dispatchable; Core/web and CLI independently pass the contracted packed cohort with canonical no-copy Environment, persisted-default/session, rollback, and Seneca proof.

title: R1.1: WorkspaceRuntime primitive, descriptor, cache, and rollback
description:
## Context
Extract the current combined runtime binding into a Workspace-owned primitive without changing externally observed single-primary behavior.

## Scope
- Define the stable WorkspaceRuntime descriptor from every R0 cache-key contributor.
- Split the current combined key into WorkspaceRuntime and synthetic-primary behavior descriptors. Replace lossy `JSON.stringify(pi)` behavior identity with stable host/plugin provenance IDs; keep hot-resource callbacks generation-scoped; distinct function-valued policies cannot alias and never create a second runtime.
- Own a workspaceId-only Promise cache with concurrency dedupe and descriptor-mismatch rejection.
- Acquire Workspace/Sandbox/adapters transactionally; rollback every acquired adapter/resource on failure.
- Keep cleanup scopes distinct: `RuntimeBundle.disposeRuntime()` releases one pair, `evictCachedRuntime(workspaceId)` evicts one provider cache entry, and adapter-wide `dispose()` runs only after all Workspace scopes retire at host shutdown.
- Make Agent factory consume an existing runtime plus one behavior input.
- Normalize omitted policy internally to default primary/allowed primary.

## Constraints
- One logical WorkspaceRuntime/Sandbox per workspaceId.
- Root, runtime mode, template, Pi, session, or actor variation must mismatch or remain invocation-scoped; never create a second runtime.
- Keep lazy creation and existing non-runtime readiness behavior.

## Proof
- Unit tests for lazy/concurrent create, mismatch matrix, exact create/dispose counts, and post-provider-acquisition rollback.
- Package layering/invariant checks.

- Unit tests prove two function-valued Pi policies do not alias and retiring one Workspace never calls adapter-wide dispose or interrupts another Workspace.

## Acceptance Criteria
The primitive is usable by the future host, has no orphan path, and preserves single-primary compatibility.

title: R1.3: generation-safe reload, leases, eviction, and retirement
description:
## Context
The shared runtime must survive real concurrency, reload, capacity pressure, and provider deletion without orphaning effects.

## Scope
- Implement staged provisioning generations and degraded shared readiness semantics.
- Track active background/request/dispatcher/tool work as non-evictable leases; make passive streams bounded and revocable with cursor reconnect.
- Bound cache capacity and implement LRU retirement only after leases drain.
- Add idempotent authorized retireWorkspace for Core/CLI remove/re-add.
- Account for control receipt before terminal settlement, duplicate receipt, queued follow-up, queue-clear, interrupt, and stop.

## Constraints
- Active work cannot be evicted.
- 256 passive streams must not deadlock admission of workspace 257.
- Provider destroy waits for retirement receipt; retry never resurrects a disposed cache.
- Disposal is exact-once.

## Proof
- Deterministic concurrency tests for generation/reload/failure and 256/257 eviction.
- Active-202 pressure, all-active prompt failure, stream resync, receipt race, timeout/destroy-retry, and exact disposal tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
Lifecycle tests prove bounded capacity without active-work loss or resource leakage.
acceptance_criteria: Lifecycle tests prove bounded capacity without active-work loss or resource leakage.

title: R2b.3a: deletion preflight, global fences, and durable retirement classification
description:
## Context
Account and Workspace deletion needs one transactional admission/fencing protocol before runtime retirement or durable data cleanup can proceed. This is the policy/journal slice only.

## Scope
- Preflight managed, dedicated, and protected Workspace policy under serializable lifecycle locks; reject with zero mutation when any app policy forbids deletion.
- Commit the global-user deletion fence and per-Workspace mutation fence/epoch only after successful preflight.
- Revoke existing auth sessions and deny normal auth/request/MCP/automation/background issuer creation or invocation consumption after the user fence.
- Fence membership, invite, create, ownership promotion/transfer, and app-lifecycle mutations.
- Classify each membership as doomed Workspace or surviving Workspace actor retirement and persist idempotent app-owned retirement jobs/receipts.
- Mint only a narrow retirement-job issuer capable of actor.retire/workspace.retire operations.

## Constraints
- This slice does not implement process-replica retirement, host session/plugin cleanup, or provider/user finalization.
- No fence, cancellation, role mutation, or deletion may survive a protected-policy veto.
- The retirement bypass cannot mint chat, tool, session, artifact, task, or plugin operations.

## Proof
- Managed/protected veto with zero database/runtime mutation.
- Existing auth/session/request/MCP/automation/background admission denied immediately after fence.
- Membership/invite/create/promotion/lifecycle race and epoch mismatch matrix.
- Doomed/survivor classification, co-owner/editor promotion policy, durable job replay, and retirement-only capability negatives.

## Acceptance Criteria
Deletion admission is race-safe and durable: forbidden requests mutate nothing, accepted requests fence every normal authority path and produce complete deterministic retirement jobs without yet destroying runtime/provider/data.

title: C3: durable idempotent typed Workspace creation and provisioning admission
description:
## Context
Typed products need explicit creation with server-stamped type and restart-safe admission; login/listing must never create implicitly.

## Scope
- Require client-generated opaque Idempotency-Key but never client workspaceTypeId.
- Persist unique operation under app, actor, expected type, and key with normalized payload fingerprint.
- Atomically create Workspace stamped with trusted type, owner membership, and operation result; replay same fingerprint, reject conflicting reuse.
- Pass durable operation ID as provider idempotency key and expose pending/ready/error retry state.
- Narrow guarantees explicitly for providers that cannot deduplicate crash windows.

- Consume the trusted `allowWorkspaceCreation` value from CoreProductRequestScope at server admission; when false, reject direct/browser/replayed create requests before idempotency-operation, Workspace, membership, provider, provisioning, or runtime effects.

## Constraints
- Current Core create authorization remains authoritative.
- No duplicate Workspace/membership/provider resource on concurrent/restart/response-loss replay.
- Full-app implicit default flow is separate compatibility behavior.

## Proof
- Concurrent and restart replay, response loss, key conflict, type override negative, exact row/membership/provider counts, failure/retry, and full-app default provisioning.

- Direct-request and replay tests for `allowWorkspaceCreation=false` assert the stable denial code and zero operation/Workspace/membership/provider/provisioning/runtime writes or calls.

## Acceptance Criteria
Typed create is explicit, server-typed, replay-safe, and honest about external provider semantics. Server-side create admission enforces `allowWorkspaceCreation=false` with a stable zero-effect denial independent of frontend UX.
acceptance_criteria: Typed create is explicit, server-typed, replay-safe, and honest about external provider semantics. Server-side create admission enforces `allowWorkspaceCreation=false` with a stable zero-effect denial independent of frontend UX.

title: R2b.3b: replica-wide runtime retirement and operation drain
description:
## Context
WorkspaceRuntime caches are process-local. A single host receipt cannot authorize provider destruction while another replica or accepted operation may still execute.

## Scope
- Register and heartbeat every Core-hosted WorkspaceAgentScope as `(appId, workspaceId, replicaId, leaseEpoch, expiresAt)` through a durable runtime-owner registry.
- Require each normal operation to validate the current owner lease; deletion fences prevent new owners and lease renewal.
- Publish the durable retirement job to every live owner, revoke passive streams with cursor reconnect, stop new work, and drain/cancel accepted request/background/dispatcher/tool work within bounded policy.
- Require every non-expired owner to retire and acknowledge the same fence epoch; allow offline owners to resume and acknowledge or expire.
- Deny stale owner resume/effects and keep provider/storage destruction blocked until all live-owner receipts settle.
- Provide the explicit single-process owner adapter for standalone/CLI.

- Branch retirement by durable classification: doomed Workspace drains every actor/owner and disposes the shared runtime; surviving Workspace targets only the departing actor's accepted/queued work and passive streams, records actor retirement receipts on each relevant replica, preserves collaborator work, and performs zero WorkspaceRuntime/AgentBinding/Workspace/Sandbox disposal.

## Constraints
- Active work remains non-evictable outside explicit authorized retirement.
- Expiry is not permission to resume stale work.
- This slice does not delete host transcripts/plugin state or finalize the Core user/provider.

## Proof
- Two live replicas, one offline then resumed, one expired owner, stale-resume denial, duplicate ack, timeout/retry, and all-owner barrier tests.
- Active/queued/passive stream drain and collaborator-continuity tests.
- Provider/storage destruction spy remains untouched until every required ack.
- Exact-once WorkspaceRuntime/AgentBinding/Workspace/Sandbox disposal.

- Surviving-Workspace tests run collaborator active/queued/stream work concurrently with departing-actor retirement and assert uninterrupted completion plus zero shared-runtime/binding disposal; doomed branch separately proves all-owner disposal.

## Acceptance Criteria
Every process-local owner and accepted operation is durably accounted for; stale replicas cannot execute and provider destruction remains impossible before the all-owner retirement barrier. Actor-only retirement in a surviving Workspace cannot stop collaborator work or dispose any shared runtime/binding/resource.
acceptance_criteria: Every process-local owner and accepted operation is durably accounted for; stale replicas cannot execute and provider destruction remains impossible before the all-owner retirement barrier. Actor-only retirement in a surviving Workspace cannot stop collaborator work or dispose any shared runtime/binding/resource.

title: R2b.3c: durable session/plugin cleanup and provider/user finalization
description:
## Context
After authority is fenced and every runtime owner retires, host-owned transcripts and scoped plugin state still live outside Core provider storage. Final deletion must journal and verify that cleanup.

## Scope
- Consume settled retirement receipts and enumerate doomed-Workspace or departing-actor data through validated SessionStore/plugin-store adapters, never raw unchecked paths.
- For doomed Workspaces, remove the complete host session tree and scoped plugin/user state; for surviving Workspaces, remove only the departing actor namespaces according to retention policy.
- Journal bounded idempotent cleanup progress and retry partial failures across app hosts without touching collaborator data.
- Preserve collaborator runtime/data continuity and unblock survivors only after actor retirement/cleanup reaches its defined terminal state.
- Finalize provider/storage and global user deletion only after every app group, replica receipt, and required data-cleanup receipt completes.
- Ensure retry never resurrects disposed runtime state.

## Constraints
- No collaborator transcript/plugin-state deletion.
- No unchecked filesystem path construction.
- Cleanup failure is a durable retryable state, not permission to finalize.

## Proof
- Doomed Workspace transcript/plugin cleanup, surviving actor-only cleanup, collaborator negatives, partial failure/restart/retry, cross-app offline resume, and provider failure/retry.
- Finalization spies prove no Workspace/user/provider deletion before complete receipts.
- Safe-path traversal/symlink negatives and exact-once terminal cleanup.

## Acceptance Criteria
Host session/plugin data and provider/user records are finalized only after validated, retryable cleanup and all retirement receipts, with collaborator data preserved.
acceptance_criteria: Host session/plugin data and provider/user records are finalized only after validated, retryable cleanup and all retirement receipts, with collaborator data preserved.

description:
Add the dedicated credential schema/store and envelope crypto. Per-workspace 256-bit DEK, AAD-bound AES-256-GCM per the research brief (12-byte random nonce, 16-byte verified tag, canonical versioned AAD = workspaceId:credentialId:providerId:fieldId:credentialVersion:dekGeneration). KmsBackend abstraction (WorkspaceKekProviderV1: generateDataKey/unwrapDataKey/rewrapDataKey/readiness, fail-closed-on-unavailable) with OVHcloud KMS as the DEFAULT managed-EU-KMS impl (external-kms-opaque.v1 payload; REST/KMIP client-cert auth; region-pinned) and an explicit LOCAL-KEK dev fallback (sealed host file, separate from DB). Scaleway/Exoscale are alternate managed-EU-KMS impls of the same interface; self-run Vault/OpenBao Transit is optional. Dev-infra Vault at 100.77.36.113 is NOT prod. Write-only/masked (last-4 metadata only), timing-safe comparisons, no-secrets-in-logs, query logging disabled for credential tables. Owner-ratified 2026-07-20 amendment A.

Acceptance gates (plan 'Cryptographic conformance'): raw Postgres inspection finds no plaintext canary/DEK/KEK/OAuth code-or-token/auth header; known-answer/round-trip per backend; corrupt ciphertext/nonce/tag/AAD/EDK/backend-ID/key-ref/key-version each independently fail closed CREDENTIAL_UNREADABLE/backend error (never 'absent'); nonce-uniqueness assertion + forbid zero/static nonce and unauthenticated modes; workspace-A row/EDK copied to B, provider-A->B, field-A->B, version-A->B all fail auth; wrong/missing KMS/local key is unreadable and never triggers another backend or instance fallback; backend conformance for OVH-KMS primary and local-KEK (sealed-file fixture, verified wrapper tag, rotation/rewrap, startup failure without key, no env fallback).

title: 16f.3 BYOK onboarding flow: provider registry + API-key + OAuth
description:
Generalize the MCP provider overlay into an owner-only 'Providers & credentials' workspace-settings surface. Provider registry + API-key onboarding (write-only forms; response returns state/version/timestamps/account label + masked last-4 only; form clears after submit) + OAuth authorization-code with PKCE and server-side refresh (local-vault custody: refresh token encrypted through the same envelope pipeline, access token memory-only; external-managed custody: store only bounded account reference, no fictitious token). Dedicated owner-authorized routes; generic /settings rejects reserved credential names. Owner-ratified 2026-07-20.

Acceptance gates (plan 'Authorization and API' + 'OAuth'): owner may create/replace/connect/disable/revoke, editor/viewer/nonmember cannot; GET/list/status/callback responses carry metadata + last-4 only (canary scans reject secret/ciphertext/EDK/token); a plain/copied/expired/cast fake cannot pass the Core authority verifier; membership removed after queue but before dispatch denies resolve; rate/body/CSRF/idempotency/concurrent-version-swap covered and idempotency stores no secret body; exact redirect/state/PKCE/short-TTL/atomic-consume/replay/cross-workspace-callback/initiator-lost-owner/denied-scope/provider-error/popup-cancel covered; refresh encrypted via envelope, access memory-only, rotating refresh atomically supersedes old version under concurrency; invalid_grant => needs-reauth without fallback.

title: 16f.4 BYOK MCP generalization onto shared mechanism (+ pi MCP edge reuse)
description:
Migrate @hachej/boring-mcp onboarding/state/server adapter onto the shared workspace provider-credential mechanism; do not ship a parallel BYOK onboarding surface. Move MCP connection authority from per-user __serverBoringMcpSourcesV1 metadata to the workspace provider-credential record; createdByUserId becomes audit-only. Consent-quarantine per decision #6: never auto-promote a personal connection; quarantine collisions; require connected-user consent + current-owner approval/reconnection. Adapt ManagedConnectorSecretResolver to the generic host-side resolver; operator Composio key stays operator-owned. Amendment E: REUSE pi's MCP connection/auth edge (pi already owns MCP client connection+auth) rather than duplicating it; inventory pi's MCP-connection surface first. Owner-ratified 2026-07-20.

Acceptance gates (plan 'OAuth'/MCP migration + 'MCP and per-consumer integration'): seed two users in one workspace with the same provider and prove neither account is promoted/selected automatically; require connected-user consent plus current-owner approval/reconnection; reject a departed-user promotion; preserve personal authority on rollback; preserve origin allowlisting, source status, revoke verification, readonly tool policy, response redaction, and secret-canary gates; MCP result passes existing readonly/redaction policy.

title: 16f.5 BYOK first-party proxy tools (Tier-1 host-side: web-search + transcription)
description:
Tier-1 host-side resolution (amendment D). Register Tavily/Firecrawl (web-search) and Deepgram/Whisper (transcription) and wire them to resolve the credential HOST-SIDE via the 16f.1 contract, make the outbound provider call from the host, and return only sanitized results/transcript/artifacts. The secret NEVER enters the sandbox. Covers the owner's actual v1 needs (first-party web-search + transcription; model keys follow the same host-only pattern). Local/public Whisper uses provider 'none'. Owner-ratified 2026-07-20.

Acceptance gates (plan 'Host and cross-tenant resolution' + consumer matrix): resolve immediately before the outbound call and dispose lease in finally; two workspaces x >=2 providers x concurrent search/transcription calls observe only their matching canaries at the final fake-provider boundary; host proxy results contain no secret in headers/body/errors/logs/events/sessions/traces; search/transcription keys never enter Sandbox exec; tombstone blocks the next resolve immediately.

title: 16f.6 (DEFERRED) BYOK in-sandbox injection (Tier-2, untrusted custom tools)
description:
DEFERRED (amendment D). The ONLY path that delivers a secret INTO the sandbox, for untrusted tenant custom tools (tenant 'user repo' code needing a credential). Delivery via stdin/pipe (FD 3) or per-execution tmpfs; NEVER env/argv/image/common-create-context. Not built in v1; deferred until a tenant actually authors a credential-using custom tool. GATED behind (a) a hostile-test harness (plant a canary secret; assert it never appears in sandbox env, /proc/*/environ, ps, argv, docker inspect/config/labels, image/history/layers, durable /workspace, logs; assert a cross-workspace probe cannot read another workspace's injected secret; mirror the runsc qualification-harness approach) PLUS (b) a red-team pass on the contract before implementation. Pairs with SBX1 wt-391-forward-6gd.3 for concrete worker-to-child FD3/tmpfs delivery + teardown.

Acceptance gates (plan 'Sandbox delivery'): two-workspace x two-sandbox cross-product rejects mismatched refs before decrypt; authenticated sandboxId<->workspaceId receipt mandatory; canary available only on FD3/fixed-tmpfs to the intended process and absent from every sink above; success/provider-error/handler-error/timeout/abort/fork/double-fork/worker-disconnect all close/unmount/clear and uncertain cleanup destroys the container and blocks reuse; remains disabled under network-none until per-provider egress + fresh SBX1 isolation/exfiltration qualification pass; positive control proves the intended tenant process can read its own credential and call only the allowed fake endpoint.
notes: DESIGN GUIDANCE (from sandbox-provider injection research, 2026-07-22): The in-sandbox delivery mechanism for Tier-2 should follow the industry standard, which corrects an earlier 'prefer stdin/tmpfs' note. BASELINE = env-var-at-creation, per-workspace, injected from the control-plane vault at sandbox boot — this is what E2B, Modal, Vercel (our current sandbox), Fly, and Cloudflare all do. Env vars are acceptable here because the sandbox is single-tenant and the secret belongs to that tenant; cross-tenant safety rests ENTIRELY on gVisor isolation strength (the SBX1.2 qualification), NOT on env-var hiding. OWASP's 'no secrets in env vars' targets shared multi-app hosts and does not apply to a single-tenant isolated sandbox where the sandbox owner = secret owner. PREMIUM OPTION (evaluate for the external-service/standalone-sandbox tier where the code author is NOT the key owner): Daytona-style egress-proxy brokering — the env var holds an opaque PLACEHOLDER and the real value is substituted at the network egress proxy for allow-listed hosts, so the key never enters the sandbox and code cannot read/exfiltrate it. This is the correct form of a 'vault broker' (swap-at-egress, not fetch-from-inside). CAVEATS to design around: (1) the daemon/injector sees plaintext at boot — minimize the trusted injector, scope decryption to boot, drop access on teardown (Fly model); store keys encrypted at rest in the control-plane vault (KMS envelope), never in images/snapshots; (2) env persists for the sandbox lifetime and is readable by any code run — accepted for BYOK (tenant's own key = residual #7), but for untrusted-to-the-tenant code use the egress-proxy; (3) persistent/snapshotted sandboxes (Vercel persistence is default) may capture env state — EXCLUDE secrets from snapshots or re-inject at resume. Still gated behind the canary/leak-invariant hostile-test harness + red-team before implementation.

title: 16f.7 BYOK migration off WORKSPACE_SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY
description:
Inventoried migration off the legacy app-wide WORKSPACE_SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY credential path (decision #11). Inventory every workspace_settings key and MCP __serverBoringMcpSourcesV1 record; classify metadata vs credential material. For a legacy encrypted credential: decrypt once with the legacy key, encrypt into the new per-workspace envelope, verify via the new resolver/provider probe, dispose, then atomically switch that workspace/provider read pointer. Never dual-write plaintext. Tombstone/quarantine migrated rows. Retire the legacy credential path only after a full rollback window and backup-retention + owner approval; it may remain temporarily for unrelated legacy settings but is never a vault fallback. Owner-ratified 2026-07-20.

Acceptance gates (plan 'Migration and rollout' + 'Lifecycle and operational proof'): verify each migrated envelope before switching reads; never dual-write secret values; rollback restores the previous safe path only and never converts new ciphertext back to plaintext; a credential already migrated is never decrypted back into generic settings; retire WORKSPACE_SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY for credentials only after backup-retention + owner approval.

title: F0a: ratify Decision-28 authority and replacement Bead graph
description:
## Decision-28 invariants
- One deployment-static validated AgentApplication fleet; no DB registry/controller/active pointer. Every fleet Agent is available to every authorized Workspace in v1; no per-Workspace allowlist. Agent identity is trusted deployment/CLI configuration; authored directories remain identity, bounded metadata, version and instructions only.
- Ordinary Workspace owns persisted default, sessions, governance and orchestration. Web membership or CLI trusted-local policy authorizes every operation. Signup hostname only initializes a new Workspace default; it never authorizes, filters, selects existing Workspaces, or mutates a persisted default.
- Core/web and CLI are independent Workspace consumers and never boot/route through one another.
- `boring-bash` owns transport-neutral governed file/search/watch/exec Environment operations and coherence; `boring-sandbox` owns Agent/Workspace-neutral physical backends. All surfaces use one canonical filesystem; no copy/sync.
- Reusable backend identity, invocation view, one-exec shell grants, and opaque model capability stay separate. Unsupported hosted physical enforcement fails closed.
- AgentApplications are service-shaped but in-process v1. No public non-default selector, remote transport/discovery, distributed tasks, or contracted-agent policy.
- Preserve authored-source safety, history/delete/ownership and lifecycle fencing. Stable errors. No file deletion without explicit written approval.

## Context and scope
Decision 26 and old R1-R6/C1-C4/I1 are rejected. Closed R0/R4 remain evidence. #844 landed compatibility `workspaceTypeId`; #845 head `e6bd0e7239eea85ed8ce867c7326a28fc2531fec` is green but semantically wrong.
- Merge Decision 28 and canonical #391/#805 plans, uniform historical banners, proof/ownership/mode/roadmap docs, and golden-path gates.
- Record #845 reviewed head/base and file-level salvage/recreate/discard destinations before closure. Keep hostname/proxy/cookie/Origin/CSRF/browser ideas; reject typed-Workspace routing/API mode.
- Recut this epic to F-series; defer old open descendants without deletion; retain closed R0/R4; create H2c/H8 and deferred H4a.
- Converge four plan/spec/architecture reviews and compact owner-authorized Fable rounds. No runtime implementation.
## Acceptance Criteria
F0A-PLAN-PROOF/PR proof records authority diff, changed-link and stale-authority scans, `git diff --check`, golden path, `br lint`, `br dep cycles`, `bv --robot-insights`, reviews, #845 ledger and Bead map. No implementation dispatch before this authority PR merges.
## Review
Product architecture, plan/spec, thermo, Fable.
## Rollback
Docs/tracker revert only; never restore Decision-26 semantics.
## Common execution rule
Run focused tests plus applicable `pnpm audit:imports`, `pnpm lint:invariants`, `pnpm check:golden-path`, and `git diff --check`. Record commands/exits/files/artifacts in the named proof. Use an isolated `.worktrees/` branch and keep the Bead `in_progress` until its implementation PR merges.

- One deployment-static validated AgentApplication fleet; no DB registry/controller/active pointer. Every fleet Agent is available to every authorized Workspace in v1; no per-Workspace allowlist. Agent identity is trusted deployment/CLI configuration; authored directories remain identity, bounded metadata, version and instructions only.
- Ordinary Workspace owns persisted default, sessions, governance and orchestration. Web membership or CLI trusted-local policy authorizes every operation. Signup hostname only initializes a new Workspace default; it never authorizes, filters, selects existing Workspaces, or mutates a persisted default.
- Core/web and CLI are independent Workspace consumers and never boot/route through one another.
- `boring-bash` owns transport-neutral governed file/search/watch/exec Environment operations and coherence; `boring-sandbox` owns Agent/Workspace-neutral physical backends. All surfaces use one canonical filesystem; no copy/sync.
- Reusable backend identity, invocation view, one-exec shell grants, and opaque model capability stay separate. Unsupported hosted physical enforcement fails closed.
- AgentApplications are service-shaped but in-process v1. No public non-default selector, remote transport/discovery, distributed tasks, or contracted-agent policy.
- Preserve authored-source safety, history/delete/ownership and lifecycle fencing. Stable errors. No file deletion without explicit written approval.

## Scope
Publish server-neutral `@hachej/boring-agent/application` with AgentApplication.invoke, immutable identity/version, invocation/session/model/Environment context, result/factory. No Fastify/UI/Core/CLI/Workspace implementation/provider values or front import. Workspace server owns and validates the frozen fleet grammar while importing the Agent application API: unique exact agentTypeId==definitionId, application default, trusted source/plugin-role references, no authored executable catalogs, no eager Workspace/App/Environment/model/backend. Host and CLI adapters compile to that one Workspace validator; Agent does not own a second fleet registry. title: gh-909 AgentGateway v0 execution
description:
## Background
Issue #909 establishes one placement-independent `AgentGateway` v0 contract and one canonical `createAgentHost()` construction path. It replaces divergent Agent assembly in Agent, Workspace, Core, CLI, playground, and delegation consumers while preserving the existing browser wire, transcript layout, authorization boundaries, plugin ownership, and runtime behavior.

## v0 scope
This epic closes only when G1, AH0, MIG-WS, MIG-CORE, MIG-CLI, MIG-PG, and MIG-DEL are complete. V0 is honest Level B: bounded event replay, process-lifetime request idempotency and effect admission, scope-bound keyset pagination, an in-process derived activity index, and deterministic no-journal rename. Durable event offsets, a durable request ledger, durable activity checkpoints, immutable snapshot pagination, recursive remote-wire validation, catalog revival, package qualification, plugin trust cleanup, and remote-host hardening remain follow-up work.

## H0/A0 dispatch gate and ready-state protocol
Graph creation is not owner approval. Before H0, this epic stays labeled `ready-for-human`, G1 stays `deferred`, and every other implementation bead stays blocked by dependencies. No child may move to `in_progress`.

At H0 the owner must approve both this plan and the #905 amendment that makes v2 a versioned additive extension of the v0 semantic core. Record the dated approval and authoritative amendment reference in this epic's notes with `br update wt-391-forward-0jpy --notes "<dated owner approval and amendment reference>"`. Then and only then run `br update wt-391-forward-0jpy.1 --status open`. Do not undefer any other bead; they remain dependency-blocked.

## Execution and branch protocol
Execute one implementation bead per worktree, branch, and pull request. Create each worktree under `.worktrees/issue-909-<bead-suffix>` from the canonical checkout and use branch `issue-909/<bead-id>`. A bead moves to `in_progress` only after every blocker is closed and the executor has claimed that bead. Keep each pull request in progress until merged.

Before owner review or merge, post a proof-of-work comment for the current pull-request head SHA. It must list changed paths, every exact automated command and result, exact manual or E2E steps and observations, rollback instructions, and any waiver or known gap. Close the bead only after merge and after the proof comment refers to the merged head.

## Dependency shape
G1 freezes the shared contract and reusable conformance suite. AH0 depends on G1 and builds the sole Host factory plus embedded Gateway. MIG-WS, MIG-CORE, MIG-CLI, MIG-PG, MIG-DEL, durable streaming core, and catalog revival depend on AH0. The five v0 migration lanes may run in parallel after AH0. Follow-up dependencies encode only genuine implementation prerequisites; follow-up completion never gates this v0 epic.

## Global v0 invariants
Shared code has no `node:*` imports or `Buffer`. App-issued scopes are provenance checked and bind current membership plus the complete Workspace/storage partition. Host identity, provider mechanics, roots, credentials, and live objects never enter v0 transport DTOs. One native session has one writer. Compatible Agents share one canonical Environment lease; incompatible placement or provisioning fails closed. Legacy routes, NDJSON framing, browser errors, prompt behavior, and all three transcript-layout branches remain byte-compatible.

## Graph and metadata proof
Before dispatch and after graph edits run `br dep cycles`, `bv --robot-insights`, `br lint`, and `br ready --json`. Expected pre-H0 result: no cycles, no lint failures, this epic is blocked by open children, G1 is deferred, and no issue-909 implementation child is ready.

## Considerations
Do not back-propagate follow-up requirements into v0. Do not remove compatibility wrappers, routes, symbols, exports, or files without the applicable recorded owner contraction approval. Do not rename `BORING_AGENT_HOST_ID` without a separately approved compatibility migration. No deletion, destructive Git operation, force push, or direct push to `main` is authorized.

## Success Criteria
The canonical closure criteria are in this bead's acceptance-criteria field and are mandatory.
acceptance_criteria:
Owner H0/A0 approval and the #905 additive-v2 authority amendment are recorded on the epic before G1 is undeferred.
Only G1 is undeferred at H0; every other implementation bead remains dependency-blocked.
G1 and AH0 complete sequentially, then MIG-WS, MIG-CORE, MIG-CLI, MIG-PG, and MIG-DEL complete with their alignment scans, exact proof recipes, and existing suites green.
V0 proof is Level B only: bounded replay, process-lifetime idempotency/admission, legacy wire/transcript goldens, multi-Workspace/multi-Agent/multi-storage/multi-actor isolation, shared Environment, deterministic no-journal rename, and bounded shutdown.
No durable-ledger crash matrix, durable activity checkpoint, snapshot-registry pagination, or remote-wire runtime event validator is required to close this v0 epic.
Streaming/durability, catalog, automation projection, #861, trust cleanup, tracer, and v2 hardening remain explicit dependent lanes excluded from v0 closure.
`br dep cycles` and robot insights report zero cycles; pre-H0 `br ready --json` contains no issue-909 implementation child.
Every implementation bead uses one `.worktrees/issue-909-<suffix>` worktree, branch `issue-909/<bead-id>`, one PR, and a current-head proof-of-work comment.

title: 909 G1 — freeze AgentGateway v0 contract and conformance
description:
## Background
G1 is the first implementation slice after H0. It freezes the placement-independent `AgentGateway` v0 semantic contract, stable errors, server-only admission and request-ledger contracts, and a reusable conformance suite. It changes no production runtime behavior.

## Changed paths
- `packages/agent/src/shared/gateway/types.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/shared/gateway/errors.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/shared/gateway/events.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/shared/gateway/__tests__/dtoDiscipline.test.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/shared/index.ts`
- Agent package export declarations in `packages/agent/package.json`
- `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/types.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/testing/gatewayConformance.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/testing/__tests__/gatewayConformance.test.ts` (new file)

## Technical approach
Define the complete addressed contract: verified and authorized scope types; Agent catalog and session references; keyset session pages; create, connect, read, rename, and delete inputs; prompt and follow-up commands; interrupt, stop, and queue-clear controls; typed receipts; snapshots; connections; Gateway lifecycle; JSON-safe event envelopes with one `seq`; and every stable v0 error.

Keep `AuthorizedAgentScope` non-serializable and issuer-owned. The verifier must reject casts, spread copies, JSON round trips, capabilities from another issuer, stale membership, and revoked membership. Reverify every operation and every command on an open connection.

Define server-only `AgentGatewayEffect`, target, key, failure, record, admission, and `AgentRequestLedger` types with exact transitions: missing to pending admission; accepted admission to in flight; strong rejection before effect; legacy observed rejection from in flight; completion; and outcome unknown. The Level-B fake is process-lifetime only.

The DTO guard is structural and type-level. Do not implement recursive runtime event-leaf validation or immutable snapshot pagination. Those are v2 and Level-D responsibilities. Parameterize pagination conformance separately from replay level and specify owner-annotated Level-D skips.

## Test plan
The throwaway in-memory fake must compile and run the reusable conformance suite. Cover scope provenance, current membership, stable error mapping, every valid and invalid ledger transition, same-request retry, conflicting digest, cross-scope and cross-target request IDs, command-state transitions, close-is-unsubscribe, monotonic sequence, bounded replay gap recovery, snapshot consistency, total session order, cursor tamper and binding denial, moved-row behavior, deleted-row behavior, and a valid cursor yielding an empty page. Assert that Level-D restart, durable-ledger, durable-activity, immutable-snapshot, and remote-wire cases are named and skipped with owner annotations.

## Runnable proof recipe
Run from repository root:
1. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent typecheck`
2. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/agent-host/testing/__tests__/gatewayConformance.test.ts`
3. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent lint:invariants`
4. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent build`
5. `git diff --check`

Expected result: every command exits zero, shared exports resolve, invariant checks find no `node:*` or `Buffer` use, and the conformance output names all deferred cases. Manual and E2E proof are not applicable because G1 changes no production behavior. Attach command results and the exact skip list to the current-head proof comment.

## Rollback
Revert the G1 pull request before AH0 starts. No data or wire migration exists.

## Considerations
Do not leak arbitrary legacy admission codes into the closed public Gateway error union. Do not confuse `PiChatSnapshot` wire status with the coarse session-list activity status. Do not add `hostId`, provider details, roots, Fastify, Pi SDK, or live objects to shared DTOs.

## Acceptance Criteria
The canonical closure criteria are in this bead's acceptance-criteria field and are mandatory.
acceptance_criteria:
The epic contains dated H0/A0 approval before this bead changes from deferred.
All shared gateway types and stable errors compile and export from `@hachej/boring-agent/shared`; server-only admission/ledger types remain under agent-host.
AuthorizedAgentScope forgery, spread/JSON copy, cross-issuer use, and stale membership fail closed on every operation and open-connection command.
The exact effect/target/key/failure/ledger state machine compiles and passes all valid/invalid CAS transition tests against the in-memory Level-B fake.
Level B conformance passes; every durable/restart/snapshot-registry/activity case is explicitly Level D/v2-skipped with owner annotation.
Keyset tests cover binding/tamper denial plus mutation behavior: moved rows may shift, deleted rows disappear, and a still-valid cursor may yield an empty page.
No recursive runtime event validator or immutable snapshot registry is implemented in G1.
All five proof commands exit 0 and the current-PR-head proof comment records outputs and named skips.

title: 909 AH0 — build createAgentHost and EmbeddedAgentGateway
description:
## Background
AH0 is the sequential foundation after G1. It consolidates the two Agent constructors and the Core hand-assembly seam behind one `buildAgentComposition()` and one awaited `createAgentHost()`, implements `EmbeddedAgentGateway`, preserves all compatibility surfaces, and establishes five reviewable checkpoints in one pull request.

## Changed paths
- `docs/issues/909/AH0-ASSEMBLY-AUDIT.md` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/server/createAgentApp.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/registerAgentRoutes.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/**` (new implementation and tests)
- `packages/agent/src/server/http/routes/piChat.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/http/routes/__tests__/piChat.test.ts`
- session, rename, and activity seams under `packages/agent/src/server/pi-chat/**`
- app-owned plugin preflight under `packages/workspace/src/server/agentPlugins/**`
- `packages/workspace/src/server/agentPlugins/__tests__/canonicalPluginId.test.ts` (new file)
- Agent-owned runtime-mode adapter exports and the Workspace re-export shim

## Checkpoint A: exhaustive assembly audit
Before implementation, commit `docs/issues/909/AH0-ASSEMBLY-AUDIT.md`. Inventory every option, hook, route, lifecycle edge, and ordering constraint in `CreateAgentAppOptions`, `RegisterAgentRoutesOptions`, and `packages/core/src/app/server/createCoreWorkspaceAgentServer.ts:840-1135`. Every row must map to Host, normalized resolver, compatibility wrapper, or app-side ownership and name a regression test. Zero rows may remain unclassified.

Proof A:
`pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/__tests__/createAgentApp.test.ts src/server/__tests__/registerAgentRoutes.test.ts src/server/__tests__/registerAgentRoutes.lifecycle.test.ts`

## Checkpoint B: single funnel and frozen composition
Implement `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/buildAgentComposition.ts` (new file) as the only harness-to-bridge-to-chat-service-to-session-store-to-tools sequence. Implement awaited `createAgentHost()` with non-empty cloned fleet validation, duplicate and unsafe ID rejection, app-owned fleet compilation, one-to-one compiler-output validation, recursive freeze, stable host identity, lazy runtime bindings, pinned session runtime identity, one writer, and a canonical Environment lease.

Canonical plugin ID validation runs app-side before descriptors or contributions are collected. `package.json#boring.id`, `definePlugin({id})`, and `defineServerPlugin({id})` must agree; omitted `boring.id` falls back to package name. Runtime identity changes for artifact digest, validated config, grants, placement, isolation, tool-contract digest, and provisioning generation. A grant-only change must preserve `provisioningFingerprint` and Environment sharing.

Proof B:
`pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/agent-host/__tests__/createAgentHost.test.ts src/server/agent-host/__tests__/fleetCompiler.test.ts src/server/agent-host/__tests__/runtimeScopeIdentity.test.ts src/server/agent-host/__tests__/environmentLease.test.ts`

All four paths are new files created by this bead.

## Checkpoint C: Embedded Gateway and honest Level B
Implement `EmbeddedAgentGateway` over the shared composition with a process-lifetime in-memory request ledger, strong effect admission, the legacy at-most-once compatibility adapter, scope-bound keyset pagination, bounded replay, snapshot recovery, no-boot activity derivation, and deterministic rename. Rename appends `session_info` to every resolved wrapper and linked-native transcript through the existing append path under one writer lock. Newest durable entry wins by timestamp, then native over wrapper, then byte offset. No rename journal, cross-process lock protocol, durable request ledger, or durable activity checkpoint lands here.

Proof C:
`pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/agent-host/testing/__tests__/embeddedGatewayConformance.test.ts src/server/agent-host/__tests__/requestLedger.test.ts src/server/agent-host/__tests__/renameSession.test.ts src/server/agent-host/__tests__/sessionIsolation.test.ts`

All four paths are new files created by this bead.

## Checkpoint D: HTTP compatibility projection
Implement the awaited Fastify projection and addressed routes. Rewrite `createAgentApp` and `registerAgentRoutes` as signature-compatible delegating wrappers. Preserve every legacy method, path, query, body, status, header, error body, heartbeat, NDJSON frame, attachment response, prompt, follow-up, queue-clear, interrupt, and stop behavior. Preserve explicit `sessionDir`, namespaced `sessionRoot`, and CLI `defaultSessionDir` branches without moving or copying transcripts. Add the in-process projected-tool conformance test proving `onUpdate` and `AbortSignal` survive schema projection.

Proof D:
`pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/http/routes/__tests__/piChat.test.ts src/server/agent-host/__tests__/legacyTranscriptCompatibility.test.ts src/server/agent-host/__tests__/projectedToolConformance.test.ts src/server/__tests__/createAgentApp.test.ts src/server/__tests__/registerAgentRoutes.test.ts`

The two `src/server/agent-host/__tests__` paths are new files created by this bead; the other paths already exist.

## Checkpoint E: adapter relocation and lifecycle
Move `createSandboxRuntimeModeAdapter` and `sandboxRuntimeHostOperations` into Agent-owned server exports and keep Workspace re-export shims. Record the `BORING_AGENT_HOST_ID` consumer inventory without renaming it. Implement idempotent Gateway close, Host drain, and Host close; cancel unbounded subscriptions; bound finite effects by `shutdownGraceMs`; fence late callbacks; dispose bindings, Environment leases, and the mode adapter once.

Proof E:
`pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/agent-host/__tests__/lifecycle.test.ts src/server/runtime/__tests__/runtimeBindingLifecycle.test.ts src/server/runtime/modes/__tests__/providerAdapter.test.ts`

`src/server/agent-host/__tests__/lifecycle.test.ts` is a new file created by this bead; the runtime tests already exist.

## Manual and E2E proof
Run `pnpm --filter workspace-playground dev`. Through the local UI, list two Agents in each of two Workspace scopes, create and connect sessions under two storage scopes, send prompts as two subjects with actor-varying tools, stop one turn, reconnect from a bounded cursor, rename a legacy transcript, and confirm no root, tool, prompt, transcript, storage, or actor bleed. Terminate the process with an active event subscriber and a stuck injected effect; confirm shutdown returns within the configured grace period. Repeat the legacy transcript fixture with `BORING_AGENT_SESSION_ROOT` unset and set to a temporary root.

## PR proof and rollback
Keep checkpoints A through E as distinct commits in one AH0 pull request. The current-head proof comment must include every proof command, manual observation, changed path, and named Level-D skip. Rollback reverts the AH0 pull request; the pre-AH0 transcript fixture must remain readable at the same byte paths before and after rollback.

## Considerations
Core and Workspace retain membership, database and plugin discovery, bridge and UI routes, asset and backend management, and trusted storage mapping. The Host never imports Core or Workspace discovery. No pre-descope durability or remote-wire machinery belongs in AH0.

## Acceptance Criteria
The canonical closure criteria are in this bead's acceptance-criteria field and are mandatory.
acceptance_criteria:
Checkpoint A commits a zero-unclassified assembly audit with a regression test for every row before implementation proceeds.
Checkpoint B makes buildAgentComposition the sole construction sequence; awaited createAgentHost validates/freezes the full fleet before serving, performs canonical-ID preflight app-side before contribution collection, and consumes only validated IDs in the fleet compiler.
The PL1 fixture changes runtime identity for every artifact/config/grant/placement/tool-contract/provisioning input while grant-only changes preserve provisioningFingerprint and the shared Environment lease.
Checkpoint C passes EmbeddedAgentGateway Level B with process-lifetime ack-loss/conflict/concurrent retry, legacy failure replay within process, single-writer isolation, deterministic no-journal rename, and derived no-phantom activity. Durable crash/restart machinery remains Level D-skipped.
Checkpoint D preserves every legacy wire/transcript/prompt byte and all three session-layout branches; delegating wrappers retain signatures; F7 proves onUpdate and AbortSignal survive in-process schema projection.
Checkpoint E relocates runtime adapters with Workspace shims, records the naming inventory without env rename, and passes bounded/fenced lifecycle tests.
Two Workspaces, two Agent types, two storage scopes, and two subjects prove no root/tool/transcript/prompt/actor bleed; compatible Agents share one Environment generation and incompatible fingerprints fail without mutation.
All checkpoint commands and manual steps have expected results and are attached to a current-head PR proof comment; rollback reads the same pre-AH0 transcript bytes.
One-machinery invariant test: both contribution kinds (workspace-activated and agent-spec-activated) resolve through the same plugin resolver, canonical-ID preflight, artifact record, and loading lifecycle; the test fails if a second resolver/loader path exists (grep-level: exactly one resolver entry module) and proves activation-site filtering selects contributions without duplicating loading.

title: 909 MIG-WS — align Workspace servers and front addressing

title: 909 MIG-DEL — align delegation onto AgentGateway
description:
## Background
MIG-DEL moves `workspaceAgentDispatcher`, `managedAgentDelegate`, and Automation's `ManualRunExecutor` from the older `Agent.send` and `Agent.stream` facade to injected `AgentGateway` operations. It also implements Automation run's durable two-step saga.

## Changed paths
- `packages/agent/src/shared/workspaceAgentDispatcher.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/workspaceAgentDispatcher.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/__tests__/workspaceAgentDispatcher.test.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/mcp/managedAgentDelegate.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/mcp/__tests__/managedAgentDelegate.test.ts`
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/manualRunExecutor.ts`
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/__tests__/manualRunExecutor.test.ts`
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/__tests__/manualRunExecutor.restart.test.ts` (new file)
- Automation run and store types required for saga receipts

## Technical approach
Inject `AgentGateway` and map delegation to addressed `createSession`, `connectSession`, prompt or follow-up send, events, stop, and close. Preserve typed receipts, stable errors, cancellation, progress, and one native session writer. Leave the old facade and duplicate buffer definitions as compatibility code for the later owner-gated contraction bead.

Automation run is a durable two-step saga. Persist the run record and invocation-to-run receipt in the Automation store before dispatch. Derive the Gateway `requestId` from the run ID. Mark the record dispatching, perform Gateway dispatch, and persist the dispatch receipt. If the process restarts while dispatching without a receipt, resolve the run to terminal `outcome-unknown` and never automatically redispatch it. Only an explicit new run may dispatch again. This safety comes from the durable run record, not from cross-restart Level-B Gateway replay.

## Runnable proof recipe
1. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/__tests__/workspaceAgentDispatcher.test.ts src/server/mcp/__tests__/managedAgentDelegate.test.ts`
2. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-automation exec vitest run src/server/__tests__/manualRunExecutor.test.ts src/server/__tests__/operations.test.ts`
3. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-automation exec vitest run src/server/__tests__/manualRunExecutor.restart.test.ts`
4. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent test && pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-automation test`
5. `pnpm audit:imports && git diff --check`

Expected result: all commands exit zero, target consumers have no direct facade or duplicate-buffer use, and restart ambiguity is terminal without a second dispatch.

Manual proof: from a local Workspace, run an interactive turn and a delegated request against the same session and verify one writer and deterministic admission. Start an Automation run, terminate after dispatch begins but before receipt persistence, restart, and verify `outcome-unknown` with no automatic retry.

## Rollback
Revert consumers to compatibility wrappers during the approved migration window. Preserve durable Automation run records and do not replay ambiguous dispatches.

## Considerations
Do not claim durable Gateway receipt replay at Level B. Do not remove compatibility symbols or files in this bead.

## Acceptance Criteria
The canonical closure criteria are in this bead's acceptance-criteria field and are mandatory.
acceptance_criteria:
Dispatcher and managed delegate consume injected AgentGateway and addressed refs; target paths have zero direct Agent.send/stream or AgentLiveEventBuffer consumption.
Interactive/delegated concurrent access proves one session writer and deterministic admission.
Automation persists the run before dispatch, derives requestId from run ID, persists the receipt, and marks restart ambiguity terminal outcome-unknown without automatic redispatch; only an explicit new run dispatches again.
No acceptance statement depends on cross-restart Level-B ledger replay.
Existing delegation/automation suites and the named restart-saga test pass; buffer definitions remain for the later removal bead.
All five proof groups plus manual concurrency/saga evidence are attached to a current-head PR proof comment.

title: 909 follow-up — migrate automation tool to class-2 schema projection
description:
## Background
Automation's existing Agent tool uses an in-process closure and is not a clean class-2 schema-projection boundary. After AH0 and MIG-DEL, this follow-up migrates it to schema projection with control-plane execution and a separate plugin-tool invocation ledger.

## Changed paths
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/automationTool.ts`
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/pluginToolInvocationLedger.ts` (new file)
- Automation operation and authorization adapters under `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/operations.ts`
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/__tests__/automationTool.test.ts`
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/__tests__/pluginToolInvocationLedger.test.ts` (new file)
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/__tests__/automationToolProjection.test.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/__tests__/projectedToolConformance.test.ts` (created by AH0)

## Technical approach
Project the Automation tool as a schema plus an actor-authorized control-plane executor. Every operation declares effect authority. Product-state mutations execute behind the scoped Automation operations adapter; current roles and permissions are reverified for every attempt.

Implement `PluginToolInvocationLedger` separately from `AgentRequestLedger`. Use stable key fields only: Workspace scope key, auth subject ID, installation ID, session ref, tool-call ID, and tool-contract digest. Pass the canonical invocation-payload digest separately to `prepare`. Detect same-key payload conflicts. Level-B storage is process-lifetime only.

Preserve `onUpdate` and `AbortSignal` through projection. Automation run remains governed by MIG-DEL's durable run-record saga; this plugin ledger never auto-redispatches a restart-ambiguous run and never widens `AgentGateway`.

## Runnable proof recipe
1. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-automation exec vitest run src/server/__tests__/pluginToolInvocationLedger.test.ts`
2. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-automation exec vitest run src/server/__tests__/automationTool.test.ts src/server/__tests__/automationToolProjection.test.ts`
3. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-automation typecheck && pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-automation test`
4. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/agent-host/__tests__/projectedToolConformance.test.ts`
5. `pnpm audit:imports && pnpm lint:invariants && git diff --check`

Expected result: every command exits zero; update and cancellation signals cross projection; mutable claims are reverified; same-key conflicts fail; and the Gateway contract is unchanged.

Manual proof: invoke the projected Automation tool through a local Agent session, observe progress, cancel one invocation, retry the same request, attempt a conflicting payload, and verify actor and scope denial.

## Rollback
Revert projection and ledger wiring to the existing closure tool. Keep MIG-DEL's durable run saga unchanged. No store migration or file deletion is required.

## Considerations
Idempotency identity never grants authorization. The plugin and Gateway ledgers remain distinct.

## Acceptance Criteria
The canonical closure criteria are in this bead's acceptance-criteria field and are mandatory.
acceptance_criteria:
Automation tool executes through a schema-projected, actor-authorized control-plane boundary with onUpdate and AbortSignal preserved.
A separate PluginToolInvocationLedger uses only stable key identifiers, separates invocation-payload digest from tool-contract digest, re-verifies mutable claims, and detects same-key conflicts.
Level-B ledger behavior is process-lifetime only and does not widen AgentGateway or promise restart replay.
Automation run restart ambiguity remains governed by MIG-DEL's durable run-record saga and is never auto-redispatched.
All five proof groups and manual update/cancel/retry/conflict steps have expected results in the current-head PR proof comment; rollback requires no store migration or file deletion.

title: 909 v2 prerequisite — remove plugin loopback and dev-bypass trust
description:
## Background
Plugin backend calls currently include loopback gates, development bypasses, and per-plugin bearer semantics. Before any remote tracer dispatch, this bead replaces those trust shortcuts with platform-owned plugin authorization context.

## Changed paths
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/hostedDueRunService.ts`
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/dueRunService.ts`
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/routes.ts`
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/__tests__/routes.test.ts`
- `plugins/boring-automation/src/server/__tests__/dueRunService.test.ts`
- Core and Workspace plugin-route authorization adapters introduced by MIG-CORE and MIG-WS
- plugin backend authorization-context contract and tests in the owning control-plane package
- MacroAnalyst backend-channel compatibility record; any external change is linked by exact pull request and SHA

## Technical approach
Replace loopback origin, development mode, and per-plugin token trust with platform machinery carrying a separate plugin authorization context: organization, Workspace, subject, roles, and installation. Scoped adapters bind partitions before plugin code runs and reverify current authority. Do not widen `AuthorizedAgentScope` with plugin fields.

Preserve Automation `/due` and `/due/hosted` scheduling semantics while making authorization platform-owned and fail-closed. Missing, stale, cross-Workspace, cross-user, cross-installation, loopback-only, and development-bypass requests must fail.

## Runnable proof recipe
1. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-automation exec vitest run src/server/__tests__/routes.test.ts src/server/__tests__/dueRunService.test.ts`
2. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core exec vitest run src/app/server/__tests__/createCoreWorkspaceAgentServer.test.ts`
3. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace exec vitest run src/app/server/__tests__/createWorkspaceAgentServer.test.ts`
4. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-automation test && pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-core typecheck && pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace typecheck`
5. `pnpm audit:imports && pnpm lint:invariants && git diff --check`

Expected result: all commands exit zero and no loopback or development trust bypass remains.

Manual security proof: call a protected plugin route with valid current context, then with absent context, stale subject, foreign Workspace, foreign user, foreign installation, loopback origin, and development mode. Only the valid call succeeds. Record the MacroAnalyst compatibility result and exact external SHA if applicable.

## Rollback
Revert the authorization-adapter cutover only while remote transport remains disabled. Never restore a bypass while the tracer or v2 traffic is enabled.

## Considerations
Trust axes are platform-granted, never author-manifest fields or IP assumptions.

## Acceptance Criteria
The canonical closure criteria are in this bead's acceptance-criteria field and are mandatory.
acceptance_criteria:
Automation and platform plugin routes use a current platform-owned plugin auth context; loopback origin, dev mode, missing/stale context, and cross-workspace/user/installation requests fail closed.
Gateway AuthorizedAgentScope is not widened with plugin org/installation data.
Macro/backend-channel compatibility uses the same platform context and any external change is linked by exact PR/SHA and test result.
No remote tracer can start until this bead is complete; rollback cannot restore bypasses while remote transport is enabled.
All five proof groups and the manual security matrix have expected results in a current-head PR proof comment.

title: 909 follow-up — activate durable request ledger and activity state
description:
## Background
The durable streaming core establishes restart-stable event offsets. This dependent follow-up activates the owner-descoped durable request ledger and durable activity state required for full Level D.

## Changed paths
- durable request-ledger adapter under `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/ledger/**` (new files)
- SQLite or file storage beside `sessionRoot` and schema tests
- durable activity checkpoint and index under `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/activity/**` (new files)
- Host startup reconciliation and `buildAgentComposition()` wiring
- Level-D cases in `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/testing/gatewayConformance.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/testing/__tests__/durableLedgerConformance.test.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/testing/__tests__/levelDUpgrade.test.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/server/agent-host/testing/__tests__/durableActivity.test.ts` (new file)

## Technical approach
Make the durable request ledger beside `sessionRoot` the Level-D default. Preserve exact prepare, admission acceptance, effect begin, rejection, completion, and outcome-unknown transitions. Replay terminal results after restart, retain completed records and create tombstones for at least 24 hours, reconcile externally accepted admission by full key, and never repeat unresolved in-flight effects.

Persist activity checkpoints with event and effect ownership. Before serving after restart, reconcile stale `running` or `aborting` records to `error`; never create a phantom or second writer.

Level-D guarantees apply only to requests admitted after activation. Activation occurs across a Host restart, so a pre-Level-D request ID retried afterward is a documented new admission. Do not implement immutable snapshot-registry pagination here.

## Runnable proof recipe
1. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/agent-host/testing/__tests__/durableLedgerConformance.test.ts`
2. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/agent-host/testing/__tests__/levelDUpgrade.test.ts`
3. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/agent-host/testing/__tests__/durableActivity.test.ts`
4. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/agent-host/testing/__tests__/embeddedGatewayConformance.test.ts`
5. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent typecheck && pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent test && pnpm lint:invariants && git diff --check`

The first three paths are new files created by this bead; the fourth is created by AH0.

Manual fault proof: run a Host on a temporary durable session root and terminate it separately after admission acceptance, after effect begin, and after effect completion but before acknowledgement. Restart after each boundary and verify reconciliation versus terminal outcome unknown. Also verify the pre-Level-D retry case and stale activity reconciliation.

## Rollback
Restart into streaming-core Level B and document that cross-restart request guarantees are reduced. Preserve durable files and do not silently reinterpret their records.

## Considerations
Transport DTOs never expose ledger or checkpoint metadata. Retention configuration may increase but not reduce the 24-hour floor.

## Acceptance Criteria
The canonical closure criteria are in this bead's acceptance-criteria field and are mandatory.
acceptance_criteria:
The Level-D default ledger is durable beside sessionRoot, preserves exact state transitions, replays terminal results after restart, retains completed/create tombstones for at least 24h, and never repeats unresolved in-flight effects.
Durable activity checkpoints reconcile stale running/aborting to error before serving and cannot create a phantom/second writer.
The crash-boundary matrix distinguishes external-admission reconciliation from true unknown effect outcome.
The pre-Level-D requestId retry case treats the first post-upgrade attempt as a documented new admission; only requests admitted at Level D receive durable guarantees.
Snapshot-registry pagination remains explicitly out of scope/skipped.
All five proof groups and manual crash/upgrade matrix have expected results in the current-head PR proof comment; rollback leaves durable files intact and documents reduced guarantees.

title: 909 follow-up — contract duplicate AgentLiveEventBuffer
description:
## Background
After MIG-DEL proves zero target consumers and durable streaming supplies the canonical event source, this owner-gated contraction removes the duplicate `AgentLiveEventBuffer` and obsolete `Agent.send` or `Agent.stream` compatibility surface without changing Gateway or browser behavior.

## Changed paths
- the current `AgentLiveEventBuffer` definition discovered by the zero-consumer scan
- `packages/agent/src/core/createAgent.ts` and related exports if still present
- focused delegation and event tests
- import and contraction scans
- no unrelated session, Gateway, or HTTP changes

## Owner contraction gate
First run the whole-repository consumer scan and attach its exact result to this bead. A zero-consumer result is only evidence for requesting approval. Before removing any Agent facade symbol, export, buffer wiring, or file, record explicit written owner approval in this bead's notes naming the approved contraction targets. If approval is absent or narrower than the discovered targets, stop without removing them. File deletion additionally requires explicit written permission naming the exact file.

## Technical approach
After the gate is satisfied, remove only the approved duplicate buffer wiring, symbols, and exports. Keep one canonical Gateway event sequence and no second replay source. Preserve legacy HTTP wire, event sequence equality, close-is-unsubscribe, and rollback through a compatibility alias to the canonical source. If any consumer remains, stop and update the graph instead of hiding it.

## Runnable proof recipe
1. `rg -n "AgentLiveEventBuffer|Agent\\.send\\(|Agent\\.stream\\(" packages apps plugins`
2. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/__tests__/workspaceAgentDispatcher.test.ts src/server/mcp/__tests__/managedAgentDelegate.test.ts src/server/events/__tests__/eventStreamStore.conformance.test.ts`
3. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent test && pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent typecheck`
4. `pnpm audit:imports && pnpm lint:invariants && git diff --check`

Before approval, command 1 must show zero target-path consumers and any remaining definition or compatibility export must be listed in the approval request. After approved contraction, command 1 must show no unapproved remaining target and all other commands must exit zero.

Manual proof: run one interactive and one delegated session, reconnect the durable stream, and verify exactly one event per sequence with no loss. Exercise the legacy browser route and confirm unchanged framing and errors.

## Rollback
Restore only an approved compatibility alias pointing at the canonical Gateway event source. Never restore a second independent live buffer.

## Considerations
No zero-consumer scan licenses deletion. Owner approval and exact target matching are mandatory.

## Acceptance Criteria
The canonical closure criteria are in this bead's acceptance-criteria field and are mandatory.
acceptance_criteria:
Repository scan proves zero `AgentLiveEventBuffer` and direct `Agent.send` or `Agent.stream` target-path consumers before an approval request is made.
The zero-consumer scan is only a prerequisite for requesting the contraction gate; it does not authorize removal.
Explicit written owner approval is recorded on this bead before any Agent facade symbol, export, `AgentLiveEventBuffer` wiring, or file is removed, and the approval names the exact permitted targets.
Any file deletion has separate explicit written owner permission naming the exact file.
After approval, only named targets are removed, no second replay source is introduced, and canonical Gateway events retain one envelope and event sequence.
Interactive, delegated, durable-reconnect, and legacy-wire proofs emit no duplicate or lost events.
All four proof groups and the manual single-event smoke have expected results in the current-head proof comment.
Rollback restores only an approved compatibility alias to the canonical source.

title: 909 v2 hardening — grants, pool, placement, and additive surfaces
description:
## Background
After the one-Host tracer, full Level-D streaming and state, catalog revival, and #861 package qualification, this lane implements production v2 grants, routing pool, placement hardening, additive surfaces, and immutable pool pagination under parent issue #905.

## Changed paths
- `packages/agent/src/server/remote-gateway/**`
- additive `packages/agent/src/shared/gateway/v2/**`
- pool, placement, grant, and service-auth adapters in the #905 owning package
- v2 files, plugin-assets, and egress projections with authorization tests
- v2 session terminal and needs-input projection
- immutable snapshot and pool cursor implementation
- remote Level-D conformance, fault, and load fixtures
- `packages/agent/src/server/remote-gateway/__tests__/levelDConformance.test.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/server/remote-gateway/__tests__/poolPlacement.test.ts` (new file)
- `packages/agent/src/server/remote-gateway/__tests__/v2Surfaces.test.ts` (new file)

## Technical approach
Preserve every v0 semantic method, stable error meaning, and placement-independent Agent identity through the approved additive v2 contract. Extend v2 refs with stable Host routing owned by the pool; never expose Hosts in product surfaces. Authenticate grants and service calls, route multiple Hosts, fail closed on pool or placement mismatch, and keep sessions pinned to their resolved immutable Host generation.

Add v2-only describe, files, plugin assets, and egress with current authorization. Add terminal and needs-input through an additive v2 session-summary projection. Implement scope-and-filter-bound immutable snapshot pool cursors with explicit expiry while leaving Level-B keyset semantics unchanged.

Run full remote Level-D conformance: historical catch-up and live tail, restart sequence and receipts, activity reconciliation, retention truncation, routing isolation, grants, placement, pool failure, and every remote-wire hostile-value category. Frozen Host generations are never hot-mutated. No pushed author code runs in a shared Host.

## Runnable proof recipe
1. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/remote-gateway/__tests__/levelDConformance.test.ts`
2. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/remote-gateway/__tests__/poolPlacement.test.ts`
3. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/remote-gateway/__tests__/v2Surfaces.test.ts`
4. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent exec vitest run src/server/remote-gateway/__tests__/eventWireValidation.test.ts`
5. `pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent typecheck && pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-agent test && pnpm audit:imports && pnpm lint:invariants && git diff --check`

The first three paths are new files created by this bead; `eventWireValidation.test.ts` is created by the tracer.

Manual fault proof: run two remote Hosts behind one pool, route multiple Agent types, restart one Host during a turn, reconnect from a durable cursor, exercise authorized and denied files, assets, and egress, mutate session rows between pagination requests, expire a cursor, attempt conflicting placement, and verify no Host identity appears in the console.

## Rollback
Route traffic back to the one-Host tracer or embedded v0 through the additive composition switch. Preserve durable stores and v0 wire; do not downgrade or delete data.

## Considerations
Reject literal-identical-v0 contract claims, Host leakage, shared-Host author code, hot fleet mutation, silent version conflict, and unauthenticated additive surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
The canonical closure criteria are in this bead's acceptance-criteria field and are mandatory.
acceptance_criteria:
RemoteAgentGateway preserves every v0 semantic method through the approved additive v2 contract and routes multiple Hosts without product Host leakage.
Full Level-D remote conformance passes, including restart seq/receipt/activity, truncation, routing/auth isolation, and pool/placement failures.
Authenticated grants plus v2-only describe/files/assets/egress and terminal/needs-input projection fail closed and never modify v0 DTOs.
Immutable scope/filter-bound snapshot pool cursors are mutation-stable, expire explicitly, and do not change Level-B keyset semantics.
No pushed author code runs in a shared Host and frozen Host generations are never hot-mutated.
All five proof groups and two-Host manual/fault matrix have expected results in the current-head PR proof comment; rollback preserves durable stores and v0 wire.

description:
